channel.title: NYT > Most Recent Headlines
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: Los griegos opinan sobre ‘La odisea’ de Nolan
item.description: Antes de su llegada a las salas, muchos en Grecia dudaban de esta nueva versión de un relato que consideran suyo. Pero tras el fin de semana de estreno, las reseñas fueron en su mayoría positivas.
item.dc:creator: Niki Kitsantonis

item.title: ¿Es así como se ve una era posalfabetizada?
item.description: No son los niños los que dejaron de leer. Son sus abuelos.
item.dc:creator: David Wallace-Wells

item.title: The World Cup in New York and New Jersey, by the Numbers
item.description: The World Cup brought hordes of soccer fans to town, and locals flocked to watch parties. But did the games deliver on promises of an economic boon?
item.dc:creator: Emma G. Fitzsimmons, David Waldstein and Stefanos Chen

media:content.media:description: Officials said the economic impact of the World Cup was on track to exceed expectations.

item.title: NYT Spelling Bee Answers for July 21, 2025
item.description: Feeling stuck on today’s puzzle? We can help.
item.dc:creator: New York Times Games

item.title: A Newsletter That Looks on the Bright Side of Things
item.description: In The Good List, Melissa Kirsch challenges readers to investigate their own lives and find happiness in places they might not expect.
item.dc:creator: Vivian Ewing

media:content.media:credit: Photo Illustration by Vanessa Saba; Arin Yoon for the New York Times, Getty Images

item.title: China enfrenta un dilema del huevo y la gallina
item.description: El país consume más huevos por habitante que casi cualquier otra nación, por lo que el reciente incremento de los costos no ha pasado desapercibido.
item.dc:creator: Alexandra Stevenson and Murphy Zhao
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Qilai Shen for The New York Times

item.title: The Grim Lessons of Israel’s War in Gaza
item.description: Israel has not been held accountable for its actions in Gaza. The nation and the individuals and countries enabling it to escape punishment may only benefit.
item.dc:creator: Omer Bartov
item.category: War Crimes, Genocide and Crimes Against Humanity
US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: ¿Cómo se podría resolver la crisis del estrecho de Ormuz?
item.description: Un grupo de investigación se basó en un pacto europeo sobre el carbón y el acero, ahora desaparecido, para sugerir la formación de una coalición de países que compartiría el control del estrecho de Ormuz.
item.dc:creator: Neil MacFarquhar
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
United States Defense and Military Forces

media:content.media:description: Petroleros y buques de carga estuvieron anclados el mes pasado frente a la costa de Omán durante un bloqueo de facto del estrecho de Ormuz.

item.title: Quote of the Day: In Tokyo Offices, a Leg-Baring Take on Business Casual
item.description: Quotation of the Day for Tuesday, July 21, 2026.

item.title: Daniel Ortega, presidente de Nicaragua, dice que planea abolir las elecciones
item.description: Ortega, copresidente del país junto a su esposa, Rosario Murillo, es acusado de manipular las elecciones recientes. Ahora, el líder autoritario dice que la nación ni siquiera celebrará una votación.
item.dc:creator: Jack Nicas and James Wagner
item.category: Human Rights and Human Rights Violations

media:content.media:description: El presidente de Nicaragua, Daniel Ortega, de 80 años, quien con su esposa ha ejercido un control completo en el país por muchos años, en una conferencia en 2014.

item.title: Trump Rejected Nearly 6,000 Clemency Applications Amid Hope of Pardon Wave
item.description: Expectations were raised for a raft of pardons in connection with the country’s 250th birthday but the president granted fewer than 20 on July 3.
item.dc:creator: Kenneth P. Vogel and Alan Feuer

item.title: NYT Crossword Answers for July 21, 2026
item.description: Kenneth Cortes thinks out loud.
item.dc:creator: Sam Corbin

media:content.media:description: See how your solving savvy stacks up against Kenneth Cortes’s clever crossword.

item.title: At Least 27 Dead in Guyana After Ferry Capsizes
item.description: Dozens of others are feared dead as crews scour for bodies after the vessel sank on its way to a gold-mining outpost in the South American country.
item.dc:creator: Simon Romero

media:content.media:description: A handout image showing rescue efforts on Sunday after a ferry carrying scores of people capsized off the coast of Guyana in South America.

item.title: Trump to Impose 50% Tariffs on Wide Range of Canadian Goods
item.description: President Trump signed orders on Monday to impose a 50 percent tariff on a wide range of Canadian exports, including wine and dairy products.
item.dc:creator: Jackeline Luna

item.title: Daniel Ortega, President of Nicaragua, Says He Plans to Abolish Elections
item.description: Daniel Ortega has been accused of rigging recent elections in Nicaragua. Now, the authoritarian leader says the nation won’t even hold a vote.
item.dc:creator: Jack Nicas and James Wagner
item.category: Human Rights and Human Rights Violations

media:content.media:description: President Daniel Ortega, 80, who with his wife has exerted complete control over Nicaragua for many years, at a conference in 2014.

item.title: Trial Date Set for Former Olympian Accused of Damaging Reflecting Pool
item.description: David Hearn, 67, who is charged with destruction of property, has pleaded not guilty. He is one of several people the authorities say vandalized the pool.
item.dc:creator: Clarence Williams

media:content.media:description: David Hearn, second from left, pleaded not guilty to damaging the Lincoln Memorial Reflecting Pool in Washington.

item.title: Tropical Storm Bertha Forms Near the Gulf Coast
item.description: Bertha, the second named storm of the Atlantic hurricane season, is expected to drop heavy rain in the region this week.
item.dc:creator: Nazaneen Ghaffar

item.title: El papa León hace un llamado al desarme nuclear en su nuevo libro
item.description: Su nuevo mensaje evita señalar países concretos, pero deja claro que el desarme, no la acumulación de poder militar, es la vía hacia una paz verdadera.
item.dc:creator: Motoko Rich
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
Arms Control and Limitation and Disarmament

media:content.media:description: El papa León XIV ha sido un crítico constante de la guerra estadounidense-israelí en Irán.

item.title: How Trump’s Immigration Crackdown Is Hurting Elder Care
item.description: Retirement communities already grappling with labor shortages are being forced to terminate caregivers at a time when more Americans are living longer.
item.dc:creator: Miriam Jordan

media:content.media:credit: Ian C. Bates for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: More than half of 400 nursing homes surveyed in 2023 said they had turned away prospective residents because they lacked enough staff.

item.title: Mamdani Eyes a McKinsey Partner to Lead Economic Development Team
item.description: Mayor Zohran Mamdani, who wanted to use the Economic Development Corporation to pursue economic justice as well as growth, is leaning toward someone likely to placate the business community.
item.dc:creator: Sally Goldenberg

media:content.media:description: Anthony Shorris, a former first deputy mayor under Bill de Blasio, is a government veteran who ran the Port Authority of New York and New Jersey, and now works for McKinsey.

item.title: World Cup Champions Return Home to Spain
item.description: Spain’s men’s soccer team received a homecoming parade on Monday after their World Cup victory against Argentina.
item.dc:creator: Cynthia Silva

item.title: A pesar del ‘falso positivo’ en una prueba, Taylor Farms sigue en el centro de la investigación del brote de ciclosporosis
item.description: La FDA dijo que continuaba investigando al proveedor como un actor importante en el brote.
item.dc:creator: Alice Callahan and Caroline Hopkins Legaspi

item.title: Will a Sex Scandal Involving Mark Lamb Matter in Arizona GOP House Primary?
item.description: The contest in a deeply religious part of the state is a test of the power of a Trump endorsement.
item.dc:creator: Jess Bidgood

media:content.media:credit: Adriana Zehbrauskas for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Mark Lamb, the former Pinal County sheriff.

item.title: Trump Orders a 50% Tariff on Many Canadian Goods
item.description: Also, Britain has a new prime minister. Here’s the latest at the end of Monday.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Cullen

media:content.media:credit: Amber Bracken for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: The U.S.-Canada border in Coutts, Alberta.

item.title: Pope Leo Calls for Nuclear Disarmament in Latest Antiwar Message
item.description: In a new book published on Tuesday, Leo said that a race to nuclear de-escalation has been “obscured by a race to armament that today frightens and terrifies.”
item.dc:creator: Motoko Rich
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
Arms Control and Limitation and Disarmament

media:content.media:description: Pope Leo XIV has been a steady critic of the U.S.-Israeli war in Iran.

item.title: 5 Found Dead in Ohio After Child Tells Driver His ‘Family Was in the River’
item.description: Two women and three men were pulled from the Scioto River, outside Columbus.
item.dc:creator: Michael Levenson

media:content.media:description: Officials near the Scioto River outside Columbus, Ohio, where five people died over the weekend.

item.title: White House Says New Air Force One Will Be Out of Service for a Month for ‘Upgrades’
item.description: The White House press secretary said the unspecified “enhancements” would take place this fall. The plane has been under scrutiny over whether it has sufficient defensive capabilities.
item.dc:creator: Tyler Pager

media:content.media:description: The new aircraft at Newark Liberty International Airport on Sunday, the same day President Trump appeared to acknowledge that it required more features.

item.title: Maine Democrats Are Replacing an Oysterman With a Lumberjack. It Might Just Work.
item.description: For the state’s progressives, Graham Platner was tempting. Troy Jackson is tested.
item.dc:creator: Matthew Yglesias

item.title: U.K. Prosecutors File Murder Charge in Ann Widdecombe Death
item.description: A 28-year-old man has been charged in connection with the death of Ann Widdecombe, a right-wing figure whose body was found in her home this month.
item.dc:creator: Ephrat Livni

media:content.media:description: Ann Widdecombe in 2024.

item.title: Trump to Impose 50% Tariff on Many Canadian Goods
item.description: The administration will use an untested legal provision to put significant duties on Canadian exports, reigniting a clash with one of America’s biggest trading partners.
item.dc:creator: Ana Swanson and Ian Austen

media:content.media:description: The tariffs, which go into effect in 30 days, would be imposed on a range of Canadian exports, including wine and dairy products.

item.title: Trump Says New Air Force One to Be ‘Maxed Out’
item.description: President Trump said on Sunday that the new Air Force One, donated by Qatar, would be taken out of service to be “maxed out.”
item.dc:creator: Jackeline Luna

item.title: Trump Attacks National Academies Over Climate Chapter in Judges’ Manual
item.description: The president joined Republican officials who slammed the National Academies of Sciences for a chapter about climate science in an educational guide for judges.
item.dc:creator: Karen Zraick

media:content.media:description: The National Academies of Sciences building in Washington, D.C.

item.title: Epidemics and Rage
item.description: Societies have always struggled with hostile reactions to health care workers during epidemics. Why?
item.dc:creator: Katrin Bennhold
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Arlette Bashizi for The New York Times

item.title: Evacuees of Ontario’s Wildfires Wait and Worry
item.description: Flames crept, and then sprinted, toward unsuspecting residents who fled remote parts of northwestern Ontario. Now they want to know why officials weren’t prepared.
item.dc:creator: Vjosa Isai and Cole Burston

media:content.media:credit: Cole Burston for The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Lyndon Paavola showing footage on his phone from his evacuation alongside community members from Namaygoosisagagun First Nation due to fast spreading wildfire that destroyed his community.

item.title: U.S. and Iran Signal Willingness to Resume Negotiations
item.description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio and Iranian foreign ministry officials hinted at an openness to resume negotiations on Monday as the countries continue to exchange blows.

media:content.media:credit: Brendan Smialowski/Agence France-Presse, Pool/AFP via Getty Images
media:content.media:description: Secretary of State Marco Rubio speaking to reporters at Joint Base Andrews in Maryland on Sunday night.

item.title: Will Frontier A.I. Labs Continue Poaching Academic Economists?
item.description: This week on “Hard Fork,” co-hosts Kevin Roose and Casey Newton talk with Erik Brynjolfsson, an economics professor and A.I. expert, about frontier A.I. labs hiring economists from academic departments —and what his poaching price would be.

item.title: El gobierno de Trump solicitó registros telefónicos de reporteros del Times y de sus familiares
item.description: El gobierno de EE. UU. está buscando de manera agresiva las fuentes confidenciales de los periodistas del Times que escribieron sobre el nuevo Air Force One del presidente. El Times le ha pedido a un juez que anule los citatorios.
item.dc:creator: Michael M. Grynbaum

media:content.media:credit: Lucia Vazquez para The New York Times
media:content.media:description: El edificio de The New York Times en el 620 de la Octava Avenida en Manhattan

item.title: Lindsey Graham’s Sister, Darline Graham, Says She Will Run to Succeed Him
item.description: Ms. Graham, who filled her brother’s Senate seat in South Carolina after his death, does not appear to have previous political experience. But she does have President Trump’s endorsement.
item.dc:creator: Emily Davies

media:content.media:description: Senator Darline Graham of South Carolina had initially been expected to be a temporary replacement for her brother, but President Trump has urged her to run for a full term.

item.title: JD Vance Is ‘Entirely Plastic’
item.description: JD Vance and Lindsey Graham both went from “never Trump” to MAGA loyalists. But the Opinion columnist Bret Stephens argues on “The Opinions” that the difference between them is that Graham held on to his core beliefs and got things done, while Vance has made a name for himself by endorsing “whatever suits a political moment.”
item.dc:creator: Bret Stephens

item.title: Anthropic’s New Ad for Claude Is a Mess
item.description: The “Hard Fork” co-hosts Kevin Roose and Casey Newton break down Anthropic’s latest ad for Claude and discuss the confusing messages of the commercial.

item.title: Despite ‘False Positive’ Test, Taylor Farms Is Still Focus of Cyclospora Investigation
item.description: The F.D.A. said it was continuing to look into the supplier’s role in the outbreak as states reported stark increases in cases.
item.dc:creator: Alice Callahan and Caroline Hopkins Legaspi

item.title: In Gaza, Spain’s Win Brings a Moment of Joy and Distraction
item.description: Many in the enclave supported Spain, which has recognized Palestinian statehood. Argentina had strong support among Israeli soccer fans.
item.dc:creator: Bilal Shbair and Claire Moses

media:content.media:description: Stringers Hub, via Associated Press

item.title: El Mayo Zambada es condenado a cadena perpetua
item.description: Ismael Zambada García, uno de los fundadores del Cártel de Sinaloa, usó un ejército de sicarios y amplia corrupción política para traficar drogas ilegales a EE. UU.
item.dc:creator: Santul Nerkar

media:content.media:credit: Jefferson Siegel para The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Ismael Zambada García se declaró culpable de una serie de cargos relacionados con el narcotráfico. Como cofundador del Cártel de Sinaloa, fue responsable de traficar drogas a Estados Unidos durante décadas.

item.title: White House Adds Jefferson Statue to the Rose Garden
item.description: The statue, which depicts the third president drafting the Declaration of Independence, joins those of Washington, Franklin and Hamilton.
item.dc:creator: Robin Pogrebin

item.title: Terremotos en Venezuela: el rescate de mascotas brinda algo de consuelo y alegría
item.description: Los voluntarios están reuniendo a las mascotas con sus familias, ofreciéndoles a los damnificados consuelo en medio de la pérdida y la devastación.
item.dc:creator: Tibisay Romero and Genevieve Glatsky

media:content.media:description: Un bombero rescata a un perro de un edificio colapsado en Caracas después del doblete sísmico del mes pasado.

item.title: What Arizona Reveals About the Future of Food Stamps
item.description: The number of people who receive nutrition assistance in Arizona has dropped significantly in a matter of months, and it may be a preview of changes nationwide. Our reporter Jason DeParle traveled to the state to understand why this happened and what effect it’s had.
item.dc:creator: Jason DeParle, Alexandra Ostasiewicz, Whitney Shefte and June Kim

item.title: Inside Andy Burnham’s 1st Day as U.K. Prime Minister
item.description: As Andy Burnham became Britain’s seventh leader in a decade on Monday, he tried to show that he will govern differently.
item.dc:creator: Stephen Castle, Michael D. Shear and Megan Specia

media:content.media:description: King Charles III welcoming Mr. Burnham at Buckingham Palace.

item.title: Teherán y Washington enfrentan un momento decisivo en la guerra
item.description: Los ataques de EE. UU. e Irán avanzan de una forma que podría provocar una guerra más amplia que no puedan contener, advierten los analistas, a menos que regresen rápido a las negociaciones.
item.dc:creator: Erika Solomon
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

media:content.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi/Polaris para The New York Times
media:content.media:description: Los iraníes, como estas personas que caminan bajo un cartel de los tres líderes supremos del país, están cada vez más frustrados con la guerra.

item.title: Faced With Piles of New Paperwork, People Are Losing Food Stamps
item.description: Arizona’s Supplemental Nutrition Assistance Program rolls have plummeted, offering a possible preview of national trends as states try to avoid new federal penalties.
item.dc:creator: Jason DeParle and Adriana Zehbrauskas

media:content.media:credit: Adriana Zehbrauskas for The New York Times

item.title: OpenAI and Anthropic Are (Still) Beefing
item.description: “These companies hate each other.” The “Hard Fork” co-hosts Kevin Roose and Casey Newton discuss the constant hatred felt between Anthropic and OpenAI and what the companies have done to mitigate it.
